title: Colorado Environmental Data Hub - Source Inventory
description: Discovery catalog of Colorado environmental data sources, cross-tabulated by theme and government/provenance tier, with default metadata and enclosure/erosion risk flags. Built as a starting point for undergraduate researchers to expand.

flag_legend.enclosure_flag: Is data privatized, paywalled, login-gated, vendor-mediated, or records-request-only? (yes|no|partial)
flag_legend.erosion_flag: Is data at risk of disappearing/degrading (federal defunding/removal, link rot, migration, obsolescence, soft funding)? (yes|no|partial)

data_integrity_caveat: Search content during compilation claimed NREL was renamed 'National Laboratory of the Rockies (NLR)' at nlr.gov with developer.nrel.gov retired May 29, 2026. This appears spurious/unverified and should NOT be trusted. Verify the live NREL domain (nrel.gov / developer.nrel.gov) before use.

sources.name: USGS National Water Information System (NWIS) / Water Data for the Nation (WDFN)

erosion_flag.note: Legacy NWISWeb pages retired by March 2026; full decommission Oct 2024-Feb 2027. Legacy WaterServices /iv and /dv APIs slated for retirement late 2026/early 2027. Data persists in WDFN but old URLs/APIs break. Timeline compressed by appropriations lapse.

sources.name: USGS StreamStats

erosion_flag.note: Federal funding dependence; otherwise stable national app.

sources.name: USDA NRCS SNOTEL / Snow Survey & Water Supply Forecasting (AWDB)
sources.agency: USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service, National Water and Climate Center
sources.granularity: Point/station (~600-800 western sites; CO well covered) + basin summaries

erosion_flag.note: Federal staffing/funding; documented data-bias note on extended-range temperature sensors.

sources.name: EPA Water Quality Portal (WQP)
sources.agency: EPA + USGS + National Water Quality Monitoring Council

erosion_flag.note: Aggregates federal+state feeds; vulnerable to upstream federal changes.

sources.name: U.S. Bureau of Reclamation reservoir/operations data (RISE / HydroData)
sources.agency: DOI Bureau of Reclamation (Upper Colorado Region)
sources.verification_note: Confirm live RISE URL (rise.reclamation.gov vs data.usbr.gov).

sources.name: Colorado's Decision Support Systems (CDSS) / HydroBase + DWR REST Web Services
sources.agency: Colorado Division of Water Resources (DWR) + CWCB, within DNR
sources.granularity: Point/structure, water district, division (7 basins), statewide
sources.temporal_coverage: Water-right priority dates from 1850s to present; ongoing
sources.update_cadence: Real-time to daily; records updated continuously

enclosure_flag.note: Core data open via API; some permanent records only via Laserfiche WebLink. API key recommended for higher limits.

erosion_flag.note: Grant/state-funded (via CWCB); web-service enhancements limited by funding. State-controlled lowers federal-removal risk.

sources.name: Colorado Water Conservation Board (CWCB) flood/hazard & water planning
Colorado Information Marketplace (data.colorado.gov)
sources.license: State terms of use (no single license)

sources.name: Denver Water GIS data & water-quality story maps
Northern Water (Northern Colorado Water Conservancy District) data
sources.update_cadence: Real-time flows; boundary updated through May 2026

sources.name: Southeastern Colorado Water Conservancy District (SECWCD)

enclosure_flag.note: No machine-readable open data; records-request only (phone 719-948-2400).

erosion_flag.note: No preservation infrastructure for public data.

sources.name: CoAgMet (Colorado Agricultural Meteorological Network)
sources.agency: Colorado Climate Center, Colorado State University (with CWCB; integrates Northern Water stations)

enclosure_flag.note: Some legacy form pages; license unstated.

erosion_flag.note: University/soft-funded; recent site redesign - legacy endpoints flagged.

sources.name: NIFC / WFIGS Interagency Fire Perimeters
sources.agency: National Interagency Fire Center, Wildland Fire Interagency Geospatial Services

erosion_flag.note: Interagency federal funding; current-perimeter 'fall-off' rules drop stale records - capture 'to date'/full-history layers for archiving.

sources.name: MTBS - Monitoring Trends in Burn Severity

erosion_flag.note: Federal; depends on Landsat program + USGS/USFS funding.

sources.name: LANDFIRE
USGS Post-Fire Debris-Flow Hazard Assessments + Burn Severity Portal / BAER
sources.agency: USGS (Landslide Hazards Program; EROS Burn Severity Portal) + USFS BAER

sources.name: Colorado Division of Fire Prevention & Control (DFPC) Wildland Fire Management / CO-WIMS
sources.agency: DFPC, Colorado Department of Public Safety

enclosure_flag.note: CO-WIMS restricted to designated personnel; public products are reports/maps.

erosion_flag.note: State-funded; 2025 preparedness plan flagged federal cuts to wildfire services.

sources.name: Colorado Wildfire Risk Assessment (CO-WRA) / 2025 Wildfire Resiliency Code Map
sources.agency: DFPC + Colorado State Forest Service (CSFS), per Wildfire Resiliency Code Board
sources.license: Application-specific (map not intended for other use)

enclosure_flag.note: Code map use-restricted; full CO-WRA data via request.

sources.name: County OEM/Sheriff fire data + Colorado Forest Restoration Institute (CFRI)
sources.agency: Counties; CFRI at Colorado State University
sources.verification_note: Thin cell - enumerate county OEM/CWPP datasets during expansion.

sources.name: NREL WIND Toolkit (Wind Integration National Dataset)

enclosure_flag.note: API requires a free key (email/affiliation); rate-limited.

erosion_flag.note: DOE/NREL funding dependence. SEE DATA-INTEGRITY CAVEAT: unverified search content claimed NREL was renamed 'National Laboratory of the Rockies (NLR)' at nlr.gov with developer.nrel.gov 'retired May 29, 2026' - this appears spurious; verify the live NREL domain before citing.

sources.verification_note: Verify NREL domain; disregard unconfirmed 'NLR/nlr.gov' rebrand claim.

sources.name: U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) wind generation/capacity
CoAgMet / CDSS climate stations (wind speed/direction)
sources.verification_note: Thin cell - cross-reference CoAgMet and CDSS station wind variables.

sources.name: University & utility meteorological towers (CU/CSU/NCAR research met; Xcel Energy)
sources.verification_note: Thin cell - locate specific repositories (CU Scholar, NCAR EOL, Xcel).

sources.name: BLM Mineral & Land Records System (MLRS)
access_method: geospatial via BLM Geospatial Business Platform Hub

enclosure_flag.note: Filing/transactions require account + pay.gov; public reports open.

erosion_flag.note: Federal; replaced LR2000/ACRES. GeoCommunicator and Navigator retired - legacy URLs deprecated (link-rot).

sources.name: USGS Mineral Resources Data System (MRDS) / Mineral Resources Online Spatial Data
sources.update_cadence: Static (systematic updates ceased ~2011; use USMIN for current US data)

erosion_flag.note: No longer maintained/updated; USMIN is the newer authoritative US source.

sources.name: EIA coal & oil/gas production
Colorado Division of Reclamation, Mining & Safety (DRMS)
sources.granularity: Site/permit; statewide (~12,500 reclaimed abandoned mines; 20,000+ candidates remain)

enclosure_flag.note: GIS open; some permit docs only via State Archives/request; spreadsheets available upon request.

sources.name: Colorado Geological Survey (CGS)

erosion_flag.note: STATEMAP relies on federal USGS National Cooperative Geologic Mapping Program funding.

sources.name: Colorado Energy & Carbon Management Commission (ECMC, formerly COGCC) / COGIS

erosion_flag.note: Renamed from COGCC effective July 1, 2023 (SB23-285); website migration - expect link-rot on old cogcc.state.co.us URLs.

sources.name: County GIS mining/aggregate overlays (e.g., Weld, Garfield, El Paso)

enclosure_flag.note: Some county data request-only or paid.

sources.verification_note: Thin cell - pull aggregate/O&G surface overlays for Weld, Garfield, Mesa.

sources.name: EPA ECHO (Enforcement and Compliance History Online)
EPA Envirofacts + Toxics Release Inventory (TRI)

erosion_flag.note: EPA proposal (Sept 12, 2025, under EO 14192) to remove Greenhouse Gas Reporting Program obligations for all but petroleum/natural gas systems; GHGRP covers 8,000+ facilities across 47 categories; 2024 proposed as final reporting year. Mirror via Public Environmental Data Partners (PEDP) and EDGI.

sources.name: EPA Air Quality System (AQS) + AirNow

erosion_flag.note: EPA early-2025 deregulatory action reportedly included air-quality monitoring curtailments; monitor for reductions.

sources.name: EPA Superfund / SEMS (Superfund Enterprise Management System)
EPA EJScreen (REMOVED - archival note)
sources.agency: EPA (archived/reconstructed by Public Environmental Data Partners)

erosion_flag.note: EPA removed EJScreen and its ArcGIS spatial server Feb 5, 2025 (per EDGI); 171 EJ/DEIA staff placed on leave Feb 7, 2025. Cite PEDP/archived copies with provenance. Colorado EnviroScreen is an independent state alternative.

sources.name: CDPHE Air Pollution Control Division (APCD) monitoring & advisories
sources.agency: Colorado Dept. of Public Health & Environment

enclosure_flag.note: Some technical docs via Hyland/Laserfiche cloud.

erosion_flag.note: State; relies on EPA AirNow pipeline.

sources.name: Colorado EnviroScreen 2.0
sources.agency: CDPHE Office of Environmental Justice (with CSU + Colorado School of Public Health)

erosion_flag.note: Depends partly on federal inputs (EPA EJScreen/CMAQ, CEJST) - some removed in 2025, which could affect future updates. State hosting lowers immediate removal risk. Excludes tribal-jurisdiction areas.

sources.name: CDPHE Open Data + Colorado Health & Environmental Data (CoHID)
Regional Air Quality Council (RAQC) Denver Metro/North Front Range
sources.agency: RAQC (lead ozone planning agency, 9-county nonattainment area)

enclosure_flag.note: Some programs funded via RAQC Clean Air Fund 501(c)(3)/grants; data public. Uses CDPHE monitor data via EPA AirNow.

sources.name: County public-health departments (Denver DDPHE, Boulder County PH) + NFRMPO
sources.agency: County health departments; North Front Range MPO
sources.verification_note: Enumerate specific county health datasets during expansion.

sources.name: USGS/MRLC National Land Cover Database (NLCD) - Annual NLCD Collection 1.0

erosion_flag.note: Federal funding; actively expanded as of 2025 (annual collection).

sources.name: USGS Protected Areas Database (PAD-US 4.1)
USDA NASS Cropland Data Layer (CDL) / CroplandCROS

erosion_flag.note: Federal funding. Resolution increased 30m->10m beginning 2024; 30m resampled version available for historical consistency; 2025 overall accuracy 75.43%.

sources.name: USGS The National Map (TNM)
USDA Census of Agriculture + USFS FACTS
sources.temporal_coverage: Ag Census every 5 years (latest 2022); FACTS ongoing

sources.name: U.S. Census TIGER + FEMA flood (NFHL)
Colorado Parks & Wildlife (CPW) Species Activity & public lands GIS

erosion_flag.note: Some layers have unknown collection dates/methods (metadata gaps).

sources.name: Colorado Geospatial Portal (geodata.colorado.gov)
sources.agency: Governor's Office of Information Technology (OIT) GIS

sources.name: Colorado Dept. of Local Affairs (DOLA) State Demography Office + GIS

erosion_flag.note: Uses Census inputs - vulnerable to federal Census changes.

sources.name: DRCOG Regional Data Catalog (Denver Regional Council of Governments)
access_method: some imagery for purchase from vendor

enclosure_flag.note: Current aerial imagery sold via Sanborn (vendor-mediated); past imagery free.

erosion_flag.note: Regional COG funding; LULC funded via a CWCB Water Plan grant - soft funding.

sources.name: Denver Open Data (geospatialDENVER)
Boulder County GIS Open Data
City of Boulder Open Data
El Paso County GIS
access_method: licensed/paid premium data (recent aerial imagery, LiDAR contours)

sources.name: Jefferson County GIS
Larimer County GIS
access_method: GIS department page (dedicated download hub unconfirmed)
